title: Mars Rover Team
subtitle: Two years of rover software work across simulation, GNSS, perception, obstacle avoidance, science tooling, and DevOps.

steps.title: Webots rover simulation environment
steps.summary: Connected the existing rover architecture to a Webots Mars desert environment using a custom URDF and world file with realistic obstacles, used a ROS bridge to connect the architecture to Webots, and simulated 14 rover software modules including object detection, obstacle avoidance, autonomous driving, and manual control.

steps.title: RTK GPS and heading accuracy work
steps.summary: Improved rover positioning to 99.8% accuracy by combining satellite GPS with real-time 10 Hz RTCM correction, worked with the RTK GPS module and initially connected it through ArduPilot Pixhawk configuration in flight controller software, and integrated an OakD IMU with custom EKF filtering to improve compass heading accuracy.

steps.title: Fast perception stack for rover vision
steps.summary: Replaced slow camera-plus-CPU detection with a faster OakD plus NPU plus ROS pipeline for real-time inference, built custom-trained YOLOv6 models, and detected mallets, hammers, and ARUCO tags with a more responsive pipeline.

steps.title: Theta* path planning over PointCloud2
steps.summary: Rewrote the rover avoidance algorithm from BFS to Theta* using PointCloud2 data, reduced rover latency by 70%, and improved avoidance efficiency significantly in constrained terrain.

steps.title: Assay analysis and embedded tests
steps.summary: Supported the science stack with data retrieval and analysis tooling for experiment results and carousel motion logic by writing algorithms to retrieve experimental data from Nile Red and Bradford assay outputs, smooth graphs, detect signs of life, and build tests for motor control and alignment algorithms used for carousel movement.

steps.title: Developer workflow and ROS2 migration
steps.summary: Helped build the developer environment and support the team-wide transition from ROS to ROS2 by creating a Docker container used by the 20+ member software team and serving as one of the primary contributors to the ROS to ROS2 switch, increasing package range and capabilities.
